title: Segfault in memory_bram/memory_map
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Using [test.il](https://gist.github.com/Ravenslofty/05d063307bcd64ef4e5e5e25977c60c3) run `yosys -p "synth_ecp5"` (for the `memory_bram` crash), or `yosys -p "memory_map"` (for the `memory_map` crash). (these are probably caused by the same underlying code).

## Expected behavior

No crash.

## Actual behavior
```
2.28. Executing MEMORY_BRAM pass (mapping $mem cells to block memories).
Processing cpu.regfile:
  Properties: ports=2 bits=1024 rports=1 wports=1 dbits=32 abits=5 words=32
  Checking rule #1 for bram type $__TRELLIS_DPR16X4 (variant 1):
    Bram geometry: abits=4 dbits=4 wports=0 rports=0
    Estimated number of duplicates for more read ports: dups=1
    Metrics for $__TRELLIS_DPR16X4: awaste=0 dwaste=0 bwaste=0 waste=0 efficiency=100
    Rule #1 for bram type $__TRELLIS_DPR16X4 (variant 1) accepted.
    Mapping to bram type $__TRELLIS_DPR16X4 (variant 1):
      Write port #0 is in clock domain 1'x.
        Mapped to bram port B1.
      Read port #0 is in clock domain 1'x.
        Mapped to bram port A1.1.
      Updated properties: dups=1 waste=0 efficiency=100
Segmentation fault
```

```
2. Executing MEMORY_MAP pass (converting memories to logic and flip-flops).
Mapping memory \regfile in module \cpu:
  created 32 $dff cells and 0 static cells of width 32.
Segmentation fault
```

This was minimised through bugpoint, but I suspect the fix for this would also fix the full testcase.
